title: Ornithogalum sandalioticum
taxonomy: Ornithogalum sandalioticum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Scilloideae and the genus Ornithogalum. Ornithogalum sandalioticum is native to Greece and is often found in dry, rocky areas. The plant's classification is further divided into two subcategories: the Kingdom Plantae and the Clade Angiosperms. As a member of the Asparagaceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as hyacinths and asparagus.

intro: Ornithogalum sandalioticum, also known as Sandalo or Sandalo Grass, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum sandalioticum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ornithogalum sandalioticum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of ointments and creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
